title: Working with Strings 🧶
description: Master text manipulation and string interpolation.

slides.title: Strings of Text 🧵
slides.content:
A **String** is just a sequence of characters like letters, numbers, and symbols.

You create a string by wrapping text in matched quotation marks—either double `"` or single `'`.

slides.title: Making Strings
slides.content: Both lines below create valid Ruby strings.
slides.codeSnippet:
puts "Double quotes work great!"
puts 'Single quotes work too!'

quizData.question: If you write `"42"`, what data type is it?
quizData.explanation: Even though it looks like a number, wrapping it in quotation marks tells Ruby to treat it purely as text (a String).

slides.title: String Concatenation 🔗
slides.content:
# Smashing Strings Together

You can use the `+` operator to combine, or "concatenate", strings.

`"Hello" + "World"` becomes `"HelloWorld"`.

slides.title: Chaining Text Together
slides.content: Notice how we have to manually add a space, otherwise the words mush together.
slides.codeSnippet:
greeting = "Hello"
name = "Ruby"

# Without a space
puts greeting + name

# With a space manually added
puts greeting + " " + name

slides.title: String Interpolation 💉
slides.content:
Concatenating strings with `+` works, but it can look messy when using lots of variables.

Ruby offers a magical feature called **String Interpolation**.

You can inject a variable directly *inside* a double-quoted string by wrapping the variable name in `#{}`.

`puts "Hello #{name}!"`

slides.title: Interpolation Magic
slides.content: Look how much cleaner this code is using interpolation (`#{}`) instead of addition (`+`).
slides.codeSnippet:
name = "Alice"
age = 28

# The clean, readable Ruby way
puts "My name is #{name} and I am #{age} years old."

slides.title: Quick Check 🧠
slides.content: Let's review the magical interpolation syntax.

quizData.question: What is the correct syntax to interpolate a variable named `city` inside a string?
quizData.explanation: Ruby uses `#{}` inside of double-quoted strings for interpolation.

slides.title: Single vs Double Quotes
slides.content: String interpolation `#{}` **only** works inside of double-quotes `""`. If you use single-quotes `''`, Ruby will just print exactly what you typed.
